commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=82156928b2f49de0852ae4da1c980efb2ac6f13b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional `deliveryThreadId` fallback to routed thread resolution and passes `preparedSessionState.sessionEntry.deliveryContext.threadId` from `runPreparedReply`.

PR surface: Source +16. Total +16 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main's ACP dispatch event still receives `originatingThreadId` from `dispatch-from-config`, while the canonical report has an ACP key without `:thread:` and a stored `deliveryContext.threadId`. I did not run a fresh live Discord/ACP reproduction, so this is source-reproducible rather than locally reproduced.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs author or maintainer follow-up because the code fix is too narrow and contributor real-environment proof is still required.

target.title: fix: resolve ACP delivery threadId from session deliveryContext
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

ACP sessions (e.g. Discord delivery via ACP) fail to route replies because `resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId` cannot extract a `threadId` from ACP session keys. The function's three-tier fallback chain ظ¤ `MessageThreadId` ظْ `TransportThreadId` ظْ `parseSessionThreadInfoFast` ظ¤ all return nothing because:

1. `MessageThreadId` and `TransportThreadId` are null when delivering through the ACP coordinator
2. `parseSessionThreadInfoFast` parses thread info from `:thread:` suffix, but ACP session keys use `agent:<id>:acp:<uuid>` format

However, the session entry's `deliveryContext.threadId` IS already set during ACP spawn (`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` adds it via `requestDeliveryHint`), and `preparedSessionState.sessionEntry` is available at the call site. The data was there ظ¤ it just wasn't being used.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix adds a `deliveryThreadId` parameter to `resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId` that slots between the inbound thread id checks and the session-key parse:

```
MessageThreadId ظْ TransportThreadId ظْ deliveryThreadId (NEW) ظْ parseSessionThreadInfoFast
```

Placed before `parseSessionThreadInfoFast` because `deliveryContext.threadId` is authoritatively set by the gateway during spawn and is more reliable than parsing session key conventions.

Only the `get-reply-run.ts` call site is wired up (the entry point that feeds thread context into `createAcpDispatchDeliveryCoordinator`). The `dispatch-from-config.ts` call site is intentionally not changed ظ¤ it has a comment warning that reading from `deliveryContext` can resurrect stale routes.

## Evidence

### Existing tests pass
```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts --no-coverage
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  31 passed (31)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts --no-coverage -t "thread"
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed | 217 skipped (223)
```

### Source-level proof
The `resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId("uses session key thread id suffix")` test continues to pass ظ¤ the new parameter is optional and doesn't affect existing callers.

The gateway already writes `deliveryContext.threadId` during ACP spawn (`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`, via `requestDeliveryHint`). The fix simply reads what's already there through the same `string | number` path used by the existing `MessageThreadId`/`TransportThreadId` fallbacks.

### Not tested
- End-to-end ACP Discord delivery with a real Discord channel (requires live Discord + agent infrastructure)

## Merge Risk

**Low**. The change:
- Adds one optional parameter (no existing callers broken)
- Reads `deliveryContext?.threadId` from an already-loaded session entry (no new IO)
- Uses the same `string | number` type already supported by the function

≡اجû Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
